---
title: "Project"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2026-03-20"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(ggridges)
library(forcats)
library(kableExtra)
data_root <- "bbl_json"
data_file <- "524915.json"
data_path <- file.path(data_root,data_file)

sample_json <- fromJSON(readLines(data_path, warn = FALSE))
overs_df <- sample_json$innings[1, "overs"][[1]]
deliveries <- overs_df$deliveries[[7]]
wickets <- overs_df$deliveries[[7]]$wickets[[5]]
target <- sample_json$innings$target$runs[[2]]
```

```{r}
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

data_root <- "bbl_json"
files <- list.files(data_root, pattern = "*.json", full.names = TRUE)

# Helper functions
clean_venues <- function(venue) {
  case_when(
    venue == "Aurora Stadium" ~ "Aurora Stadium, Launceston",
    venue == "Brisbane Cricket Ground" ~ "Brisbane Cricket Ground, Woolloongabba, Brisbane",
    venue == "Brisbane Cricket Ground, Woolloongabba" ~ "Brisbane Cricket Ground, Woolloongabba, Brisbane",
    venue == "Bellerive Oval" ~ "Bellerive Oval, Hobart",
    venue == "Docklands Stadium" ~ "Docklands Stadium, Melbourne",
    venue == "Geelong Cricket Ground" ~ "GMHBA Stadium, South Geelong, Victoria",
    venue == "Simonds Stadium, South Geelong, Victoria" ~ "GMHBA Stadium, South Geelong, Victoria",
    venue == "International Sports Stadium" ~ "International Sports Stadium, Coffs Harbour",
    venue == "Manuka Oval" ~ "Manuka Oval, Canberra",
    venue == "W.A.C.A. Ground" ~ "Western Australia Cricket Association Ground",
    venue == "Sydney Cricket Ground" ~ "Sydney Cricket Ground, Sydney",
    TRUE ~ venue
  )
}

get_home_team <- function(info) {
  venue <- info$venue
  teams <- info$teams
  if (venue == "Adelaide Oval" && "Adelaide Strikers" %in% teams) "Adelaide Strikers"
  else if (venue == "Aurora Stadium, Launceston" && "Hobart Hurricanes" %in% teams) "Hobart Hurricanes"
  else if (venue == "Bellerive Oval, Hobart" && "Hobart Hurricanes" %in% teams) "Hobart Hurricanes"
  else if (venue == "Brisbane Cricket Ground, Woolloongabba, Brisbane" && "Brisbane Heat" %in% teams) "Brisbane Heat"
  else if (venue == "Docklands Stadium, Melbourne" && "Melbourne Renegades" %in% teams) "Melbourne Renegades"
  else if (venue == "Carrara Oval" && "Brisbane Heat" %in% teams) "Brisbane Heat"
  else if (venue == "GMHBA Stadium, South Geelong, Victoria" && "Melbourne Renegades" %in% teams) "Melbourne Renegades"
  else if (venue == "Melbourne Cricket Ground" && "Melbourne Stars" %in% teams) "Melbourne Stars"
  else if (venue == "Melbourne Cricket Ground" && "Melbourne Renegades" %in% teams) "Melbourne Renegades"
  else if (venue == "Perth Stadium" && "Perth Scorchers" %in% teams) "Perth Scorchers"
  else if (venue == "Stadium Australia" && "Sydney Sixers" %in% teams) "Sydney Sixers"
  else if (venue == "Stadium Australia" && "Sydney Thunder" %in% teams) "Sydney Thunder"
  else if (venue == "Sydney Cricket Ground, Sydney" && "Sydney Sixers" %in% teams) "Sydney Sixers"
  else if (venue == "Sydney Cricket Ground, Sydney" && "Sydney Thunder" %in% teams) "Sydney Thunder"
  else if (venue == "Sydney Showground Stadium" && "Sydney Thunder" %in% teams) "Sydney Thunder"
  else if (venue == "Sydney Showground Stadium" && "Sydney Sixers" %in% teams) "Sydney Sixers"
  else if (venue == "Manuka Oval, Canberra" && "Sydney Thunder" %in% teams) "Sydney Thunder"
  else if (venue == "Manuka Oval, Canberra" && "Sydney Sixers" %in% teams) "Sydney Sixers"
  else if (venue == "Western Australia Cricket Association Ground" && "Perth Scorchers" %in% teams) "Perth Scorchers"
  else if (venue == "University of Tasmania Stadium, Launceston" && "Hobart Hurricanes" %in% teams) "Hobart Hurricanes"
  else NA_character_
}
```

```{r}
playing_xi <- map_dfr(files, function(file) {
  tryCatch({
    json <- fromJSON(readLines(file, warn = FALSE))
    info <- json$info
    match_id <- as.character(str_remove(basename(file), ".json"))
    
    # Extract team names and players
    teams <- info$teams
    players_list <- info$players
    
    # Flatten into long format
    map_dfr(teams, function(team_name) {
      tibble(
        match_id = match_id,
        team = team_name,
        player = players_list[[team_name]]
      )
    })
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error extracting XI from ", file, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
    return(tibble())
  })
})

# Preview
playing_xi %>% filter(match_id == unique(playing_xi$match_id)[1])


target_df <- map_dfr(files, function(file) {
  tryCatch({
    json <- fromJSON(readLines(file, warn = FALSE))
    match_id <- str_remove(basename(file), ".json")
    
    # Extract target from second innings if available
    target_runs <- json$innings$target$runs[2] %||% NA_integer_
    
    tibble(
      match_id = match_id,
      target = target_runs
    )
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error extracting target from ", file, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
    return(tibble())
  })
})

```

```{r}
balls <- map_dfr(files, function(file) {
  tryCatch({
    json <- fromJSON(readLines(file, warn = FALSE))
    info <- json$info
    info$venue <- clean_venues(info$venue)
    # --- Extract target score if available ---
    target_runs <- tryCatch({
      json$innings$target$runs[[2]] %||% NA_integer_
    }, error = function(e) NA_integer_)
    # --- Match-level metadata ---
    row_meta <- tibble(
      match_id = as.character(str_remove(basename(file), ".json")),
      date = as.character(info$dates[[1]]),
      team1 = as.character(info$teams[[1]]),
      team2 = as.character(info$teams[[2]]),
      home_team = as.character(get_home_team(info)),
      winner = as.character(info$outcome$winner %||% NA_character_),
      result = as.character(info$outcome$result %||% NA_character_),
      winner_by_runs = as.integer(info$outcome$by$runs %||% NA_integer_),
      winner_by_wickets = as.integer(info$outcome$by$wickets %||% NA_integer_),
      venue = as.character(info$venue),
      target = target_runs
    )
    
    # --- Innings-level iteration ---
    map_dfr(1:nrow(json$innings), function(i) {
      team <- as.character(json$innings[i, "team"][[1]])
      overs_df <- json$innings[i, "overs"][[1]]
      
      # --- Over-level iteration ---
      map_dfr(1:nrow(overs_df), function(o) {
        deliveries <- overs_df$deliveries[[o]]
        over_num <- as.integer(overs_df$over[o])
        
        # --- Delivery-level flattening ---
        deliveries_flat <- deliveries %>%
          mutate(
            innings = as.integer(i),
            team = as.character(team),
            over = over_num,
            ball_in_over = row_number(),
            delivery = as.numeric(sprintf("%d.%d", over_num, ball_in_over)),
            batter = as.character(batter %||% NA_character_),
            bowler = as.character(bowler %||% NA_character_),
            non_striker = as.character(non_striker %||% NA_character_),
            runs_batter = as.integer(runs$batter %||% 0),
            runs_extras = as.integer(runs$extras %||% 0),
            runs_total = as.integer(runs$total %||% 0),
            powerplay = as.logical(over_num < 6),
            powerplay_type = as.character(if (over_num < 6) "Mandatory" else NA_character_)
          )
        
        # --- Wicket logic safely extracted row-wise ---
        wicket_info <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(deliveries)), function(b) {
          wkts <- deliveries$wickets[[b]]
          tibble(
            wicket = !is.null(wkts),
            wicket_type = if (!is.null(wkts)) wkts$kind[1] else NA_character_,
            wicket_player_out = if (!is.null(wkts)) wkts$player_out[1] else NA_character_
          )
        })
        
        # --- Combine safely ---
        bind_cols(deliveries_flat, wicket_info)
      })
    }) %>% bind_cols(row_meta[rep(1, nrow(.)), ])
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error processing ", file, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
    return(tibble())
  })
})

balls <- balls %>% arrange(date,match_id, innings, delivery)
head(balls)



```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# --- Batting scorecard ---
batting_scorecard <- balls %>%
  group_by(match_id, batter) %>%
  summarise(
    balls_faced = n(),
    runs = sum(runs_batter, na.rm = TRUE),
    fours = sum(runs_batter == 4, na.rm = TRUE),
    sixes = sum(runs_batter == 6, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(match_id, desc(runs))

# --- Bowling scorecard ---
bowling_scorecard <- balls %>%
  group_by(match_id, bowler) %>%
  summarise(
    balls_bowled = n(),
    overs = balls_bowled %/% 6 + (balls_bowled %% 6) / 10,
    dot_balls = sum(runs_total == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    wickets = sum(wicket & !is.na(wicket_player_out), na.rm = TRUE),
    runs_conceded = sum(runs_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    runs_extras = sum(runs_extras, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(match_id, desc(wickets))


# --- View one match example ---
match_example <- unique(balls$match_id)[1]

batting_scorecard %>% filter(match_id == match_example)
bowling_scorecard %>% filter(match_id == match_example)
```

```{r}
snapshot_df <- balls %>%
  filter(innings == 2) %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  mutate(
    balls_bowled = row_number(),
    overs_completed = floor(balls_bowled / 6) + (balls_bowled %% 6) / 10,
    current_runs = cumsum(runs_total),
    wickets_lost = cumsum(wicket),
    balls_remaining = 120 - balls_bowled,
    run_rate = current_runs / overs_completed,
    required_run_rate = if_else(innings == 2 & !is.na(target),
                            (target - current_runs) / (balls_remaining / 6),
                            NA_real_),
    batting_team = team,
    bowling_team = if_else(team == team1, team2, team1),
    target = target
  ) %>%
  filter(overs_completed >= 13) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%  # First delivery after 13th over
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    remaining_wickets = 10 - wickets_lost,
    match_winner = if_else(batting_team == winner, 1, 0),
    
  ) %>%
  select(
    match_id,
    date,
    venue,
    batting_team,
    bowling_team,
    target,
    current_runs,
    wickets_lost,
    remaining_wickets,
    balls_bowled,
    balls_remaining,
    overs_completed,
    run_rate,
    required_run_rate,
    powerplay,
    home_team,
    winner,
    match_winner
  )


```



```{r}
library(caret)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(pROC)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)
library(dplyr)

# Step 1: Prepare data
model_data <- snapshot_df %>%
  mutate(across(c(batting_team, bowling_team, venue, home_team), as.factor),
         match_winner = as.factor(match_winner)) %>%
  drop_na(match_winner)

# Step 2: Train-test split
set.seed(42)
train_index <- createDataPartition(model_data$match_winner, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- model_data[train_index, ]
test_data <- model_data[-train_index, ]

# Step 3: Align factor levels
for (col in c("batting_team", "bowling_team", "venue", "home_team")) {
  test_data[[col]] <- factor(test_data[[col]], levels = levels(train_data[[col]]))
}

# Step 4: Drop rows with unseen levels (now NA)
test_data <- test_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(batting_team), !is.na(bowling_team), !is.na(venue), !is.na(home_team))

# Step 5: Logistic regression model
log_model <- glm(match_winner ~ current_runs + wickets_lost + remaining_wickets +
                   balls_remaining + overs_completed + run_rate + required_run_rate +
                   powerplay + batting_team + bowling_team + venue + home_team,
                 data = train_data, family = binomial)

summary(log_model)

# Step 6: Predict and evaluate
log_probs <- predict(log_model, test_data, type = "response")
log_pred <- ifelse(log_probs > 0.5, 1, 0)
confusionMatrix(factor(log_pred), test_data$match_winner)

# Step 7: Decision tree model
tree_model <- rpart(match_winner ~ current_runs + wickets_lost + remaining_wickets +
                      balls_remaining + overs_completed + run_rate + required_run_rate +
                      powerplay + batting_team + bowling_team + venue + home_team,
                    data = train_data, method = "class", control = rpart.control(cp = 0.01))

tree_pred <- predict(tree_model, test_data, type = "class")
confusionMatrix(tree_pred, test_data$match_winner)

# Step 8: Visualize decision tree
rpart.plot(tree_model, type = 2, extra = 104, fallen.leaves = TRUE)

# Step 9: ROC curve and AUC
library(pROC)

# Compute ROC
roc_curve <- roc(test_data$match_winner, log_probs)

# Extract AUC
auc_value <- auc(roc_curve)

plot(
  roc_curve,
  col = "#1f78b4",          
  lwd = 3,                  
  main = "ROC Curve - Logistic Regression",
  cex.main = 1.3,
  cex.lab = 1.2,
  cex.axis = 1.1,
  legacy.axes = TRUE        # keeps 0–1 axes
)

# Add diagonal reference line
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "gray60", lwd = 2)

# Add grid
grid(col = "gray90", lwd = 1, lty = 1)

# Add AUC text box
legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = paste("AUC =", round(auc_value, 3)),
  bty = "n",
  text.col = "#1f78b4",
  cex = 1.2
)

# Step 10: Variable importance (logistic regression)
tidy(log_model) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(abs_estimate = abs(estimate)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(term, abs_estimate), y = abs_estimate)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Variable Importance (Logistic Regression)",
       x = "Predictor", y = "Absolute Coefficient")


```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# Prepare bowling summary consistent with batting_scorecard naming

bowling_summary <- bowling_scorecard %>%
rename(balls_bowled = balls_bowled, runs_conceded = runs_conceded, wickets_taken = wickets) %>%
select(match_id, bowler, balls_bowled, overs, wickets_taken, runs_conceded) %>%
group_by(bowler) %>%
summarise(
balls_bowled = sum(balls_bowled, na.rm = TRUE),
wickets = sum(wickets_taken, na.rm = TRUE),
runs_conceded = sum(runs_conceded, na.rm = TRUE),
.groups = "drop"
) %>%
rename(player = bowler)

# Prepare batting summary

batting_summary <- batting_scorecard %>%
group_by(batter) %>%
summarise(
balls_faced = sum(balls_faced, na.rm = TRUE),
runs = sum(runs, na.rm = TRUE),
fours = sum(fours, na.rm = TRUE),
sixes = sum(sixes, na.rm = TRUE),
.groups = "drop"
) %>%
rename(player = batter)

# Combine batting + bowling (players may appear in only one)

player_stats <- full_join(batting_summary, bowling_summary, by = "player") %>%
replace_na(list(
balls_faced = 0,
runs = 0,
fours = 0,
sixes = 0,
balls_bowled = 0,
wickets = 0,
runs_conceded = 0
))

# Convert balls_bowled to overs_decimal for a readable value

player_stats <- player_stats %>%
mutate(
overs_bowled = balls_bowled / 6,
# Classification rules (allrounder first)
role = case_when(
balls_faced >= 20 & balls_bowled >= 12 ~ "Allrounder",       # >=2 overs (12 balls) and >=20 balls faced
balls_bowled >= 18 & !(balls_faced >= 20 & balls_bowled >= 12) ~ "Bowler",  # >=3 overs (18 balls) & not allrounder
balls_faced >= 20 & !(balls_faced >= 20 & balls_bowled >= 12) ~ "Batter",   # >=20 balls faced & not allrounder
TRUE ~ "Other"
)
)

# Inspect top few

player_stats %>% arrange(desc(role), desc(runs)) %>% slice_head(n = 20)


# 1) Count of players by role

role_counts <- player_stats %>% count(role) %>% arrange(desc(n))
ggplot(role_counts, aes(x = reorder(role, n), y = n, fill = role)) +
geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
coord_flip() +
labs(title = "Number of Players by Role (based on thresholds)",
x = "Role", y = "Players") +
theme_minimal(base_size = 12)




# 3) Top players in each role (by a sensible metric)

top_n <- 12

# Top batters by runs

top_batters <- player_stats %>% filter(role == "Batter" | role == "Allrounder") %>% arrange(desc(runs)) %>% slice_head(n = top_n) %>% mutate(metric = runs)

# Top bowlers by wickets

top_bowlers <- player_stats %>% filter(role == "Bowler" | role == "Allrounder") %>% arrange(desc(wickets)) %>% slice_head(n = top_n) %>% mutate(metric = wickets)

# Combine and plot as facet

top_combined <- bind_rows(
top_batters %>% mutate(kind = "Top Batters (by runs)"),
top_bowlers %>% mutate(kind = "Top Bowlers (by wickets)")
) %>%
mutate(player = forcats::fct_reorder(player, metric))



# Optional: produce a small summary table and save for inspection

role_summary_table <- player_stats %>%
arrange(desc(role), desc(runs)) %>%
select(player, role, runs, balls_faced, wickets, balls_bowled, overs_bowled)



```



```{r}
# --- Decision tree ROC & AUC ---
# 1) Predicted probabilities from rpart (probability of class "1")
# ensure tree_model is the fitted rpart object and test_data is your test set
tree_probs <- predict(tree_model, test_data, type = "prob")[, "1"]

# 2) Prepare true labels as numeric 0/1 (safe conversion)
if (is.factor(test_data$match_winner)) {
  # try straightforward conversion if levels are "0"/"1"
  true_labels <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(test_data$match_winner)))
} else {
  true_labels <- as.numeric(test_data$match_winner)
}

# fallback mapping if above produced NA (e.g., "loss"/"win" labels)
if (any(is.na(true_labels))) {
  levs <- unique(as.character(test_data$match_winner))
  message("Detected non-numeric match_winner levels: ", paste(levs, collapse = ", "))
  # adjust the mapping below to match your factor labels if different
  true_labels <- ifelse(as.character(test_data$match_winner) %in% c("1", "win", "Win", "WIN"), 1, 0)
}

# 3)checks
stopifnot(length(tree_probs) == length(true_labels))
if (length(unique(true_labels)) < 2) stop("true_labels has only one class — cannot compute ROC.")
if (all(is.na(tree_probs))) stop("tree_probs are all NA — check predict() call.")

# 4) Compute ROC and AUC
roc_tree <- roc(response = true_labels, predictor = tree_probs, quiet = TRUE)
auc_tree <- auc(roc_tree)
cat("Decision tree AUC:", round(auc_tree, 4), "\n")




```

```{r}

# 1A: Balls faced by role — Ridgeline plot (filtered & cleaner)
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other", balls_faced > 0),
       aes(x = balls_faced, y = role, fill = role)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    alpha          = 0.85,
    scale          = 1.2,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    quantile_lines = TRUE,
    quantiles      = 2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Batter"     = "#1D9E75",
    "Bowler"     = "#378ADD",
    "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27"
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    limits = c(0, 2500),          # clip long tail — covers 95%+ of players
    breaks = seq(0, 1500, 250)    # clean tick marks every 250
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Distribution of balls faced by player role",
    subtitle = "Median line shown | x-axis capped at 2,500 balls to improve readability",
    x        = "Balls faced (career total)",
    y        = "Player role"
  ) +
  theme_ridges(font_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title      = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey50", margin = margin(b = 10)),
    axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()   # removes minor gridlines for cleaner look
  )
```

```{r}
# 1B: Balls bowled by role — Ridgeline plot (fixed overlap)
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other", balls_bowled > 0),
       aes(x = balls_bowled, y = role, fill = role)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    alpha          = 0.75,
    scale          = 0.9,          # reduced from 1.2 — prevents overlap
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    quantile_lines = TRUE,
    quantiles      = 2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Batter"     = "#1D9E75",
    "Bowler"     = "#378ADD",
    "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27"
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    limits = c(0, 1500),
    breaks = seq(0, 1500, 250)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(0.5, 1.2))) +  # more vertical breathing room
  labs(
    title    = "Distribution of balls bowled by player role",
    subtitle = "Median line shown | x-axis capped at 1,500 balls to improve readability",
    x        = "Balls bowled (career total)",
    y        = "Player role"
  ) +
  theme_ridges(font_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    plot.title       = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey50", margin = margin(b = 10)),
    axis.title.x     = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r}

# ============================================================
# STEP 1A: Compute average batting position per player
# ============================================================

batting_position <- balls %>%
  filter(innings %in% c(1, 2)) %>%
  group_by(match_id, innings, batter) %>%
  summarise(first_ball = min(delivery), .groups = "drop") %>%
  # Rank batters within each innings by when they first faced a ball
  group_by(match_id, innings) %>%
  mutate(batting_position = rank(first_ball, ties.method = "first")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(batter) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_batting_position = round(mean(batting_position, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    matches_batted       = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(player = batter)

# Preview
batting_position %>% arrange(avg_batting_position) %>% head(10)

# ============================================================
# STEP 1B: Position weight — top-order worth more than tail
# ============================================================
# Weight formula: position 1 = 1.0, position 11 = 0.0
# Linear decay: w = (11 - avg_position) / 10
# This means:
#   Position 1  → weight 1.0
#   Position 5  → weight 0.6
#   Position 8  → weight 0.3
#   Position 11 → weight 0.0

batting_position <- batting_position %>%
  mutate(
    position_weight = (11 - avg_batting_position) / 10,
    position_weight = pmax(pmin(position_weight, 1), 0) # clamp to [0, 1]
  )

# ============================================================
# STEP 1C: Merge position weight into player_stats and
#           compute position-adjusted W_batter
# ============================================================

player_stats <- player_stats %>%
  left_join(batting_position %>%
              select(player, avg_batting_position, position_weight),
            by = "player") %>%
  mutate(
    # Fill missing position weight with 0 (never batted)
    position_weight = replace_na(position_weight, 0),

    strike_rate = ifelse(balls_faced > 0,
                         (runs / balls_faced) * 100,
                         NA_real_),

    # Raw score = SR × position weight
    # Players who never batted get 0
    sr_position_raw = case_when(
      balls_faced > 0 ~ strike_rate * position_weight,
      TRUE            ~ 0
    ),

    # Normalise to 0-1 =
    W_batter = case_when(
      balls_faced > 0 ~
        (sr_position_raw - min(sr_position_raw[balls_faced > 0], na.rm = TRUE)) /
        (max(sr_position_raw[balls_faced > 0], na.rm = TRUE) -
         min(sr_position_raw[balls_faced > 0], na.rm = TRUE)),
      balls_faced == 0 ~ 0
    )
  )

# ============================================================
# SANITY CHECK — position-adjusted W_batter
# ============================================================

# Should show top-order high-SR players ranked highest
player_stats %>%
  filter(balls_faced > 0) %>%
  select(player, role, avg_batting_position, position_weight,
         strike_rate, sr_position_raw, W_batter) %>%
  arrange(desc(W_batter)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  print()

# Compare: same player's old vs new score
# A tail-end slogger (pos 10) with high SR should now rank LOWER
# than a top-order batter (pos 3) with similar SR
player_stats %>%
  filter(balls_faced > 0) %>%
  select(player, role, avg_batting_position, strike_rate, W_batter) %>%
  arrange(desc(strike_rate)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  print()

# Summary by role — mean position weight
player_stats %>%
  filter(role != "Other") %>%
  group_by(role) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_avg_position    = round(mean(avg_batting_position, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    mean_position_weight = round(mean(position_weight,      na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    mean_W_batter        = round(mean(W_batter,             na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  print()


# Step 2: Full view including bowlers
batter_weights <- player_stats %>%
  filter(role != "Other") %>%
  select(player, role, balls_faced, runs, strike_rate, W_batter) %>%
  arrange(desc(W_batter))

# Preview top 20
batter_weights %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  print()

# Preview bowlers specifically — to verify 0s and non-zero weights
batter_weights %>%
  filter(role == "Bowler") %>%
  arrange(desc(W_batter)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  print()


# Step 3: Plot — now shows all three roles
ggplot(batter_weights %>% filter(role != "Other"),
       aes(x = W_batter, fill = role)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~role, ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Batter"     = "#1D9E75",
    "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27",
    "Bowler"     = "#378ADD"
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Batter weightage distribution (W_batter) by role",
    subtitle = "Batters/Allrounders normalised by SR | Bowlers: 0 if never batted, SR-based if they have",
    x        = "Batter weight (0 = no batting contribution, 1 = highest SR)",
    y        = "Number of players",
    fill     = "Role"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position  = "none"
  )


# Step 4: Summary statistics per role for report
player_stats %>%
  filter(role != "Other") %>%
  group_by(role) %>%
  summarise(
    n                  = n(),
    batted             = sum(balls_faced > 0),
    never_batted       = sum(balls_faced == 0),
    mean_SR            = round(mean(strike_rate, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    median_SR          = round(median(strike_rate, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    mean_W_batter      = round(mean(W_batter, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups            = "drop"
  ) %>%
  print()
 
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# BOWLER WEIGHTAGE 
# ============================================================

# Step 1: Compute raw metrics for all players
player_stats <- player_stats %>%
  mutate(
    # Economy rate: runs per over (NA if never bowled)
    economy_rate = ifelse(balls_bowled > 0,
                          (runs_conceded / balls_bowled) * 6,
                          NA_real_),

    # Bowling SR: balls per wicket (NA if no wickets taken)
    bowling_sr   = ifelse(wickets > 0,
                          balls_bowled / wickets,
                          NA_real_)
  )

# Step 2: Invert both metrics
# Separate mutate so max() reliably references finalised columns from Step 1
player_stats <- player_stats %>%
  mutate(
    # Invert economy: best bowler (lowest economy) gets highest score
    # Players who never bowled get 0
    economy_score = ifelse(
      balls_bowled > 0,
      max(economy_rate, na.rm = TRUE) - economy_rate,
      0
    ),

    # Invert bowling SR: fastest wicket-taker gets highest score
    # Players with 0 wickets get 0
    bowling_sr_score = ifelse(
      !is.na(bowling_sr),
      max(bowling_sr, na.rm = TRUE) - bowling_sr,
      0
    )
  )

# Step 3: Normalise and compute final W_bowler
# Separate mutate so normalisation references finalised scores from Step 2
player_stats <- player_stats %>%
  mutate(
    # Normalise economy score to 0–1
    # Players who never bowled get 0
    econ_norm = ifelse(
      balls_bowled > 0,
      (economy_score - min(economy_score[balls_bowled > 0], na.rm = TRUE)) /
      (max(economy_score[balls_bowled > 0], na.rm = TRUE) -
       min(economy_score[balls_bowled > 0], na.rm = TRUE)),
      0
    ),

    # Normalise bowling SR score to 0–1
    # Players with no wickets get 0
    sr_norm = ifelse(
      !is.na(bowling_sr),
      (bowling_sr_score - min(bowling_sr_score[!is.na(bowling_sr)], na.rm = TRUE)) /
      (max(bowling_sr_score[!is.na(bowling_sr)], na.rm = TRUE) -
       min(bowling_sr_score[!is.na(bowling_sr)], na.rm = TRUE)),
      0
    ),

    # Final W_bowler: economy 50% + bowling SR 50%
    # All roles:
    # Bowlers/Allrounders who bowl  — proper weighted score
    # Batters who have bowled       — score on same scale
    # Players who never bowled      — 0
    W_bowler = 0.5 * econ_norm + 0.5 * sr_norm
  )


# ============================================================
# FILTER VIEW FOR INSPECTION
# ============================================================

bowler_weights <- player_stats %>%
  filter(role != "Other") %>%
  select(player, role, balls_bowled, wickets, runs_conceded,
         economy_rate, bowling_sr,
         economy_score, bowling_sr_score,
         econ_norm, sr_norm, W_bowler) %>%
  arrange(desc(W_bowler))

# Preview top 20
bowler_weights %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  print()


# ============================================================
# PLOTS
# ============================================================

# Plot 1: W_bowler distribution by role
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other"),
       aes(x = W_bowler, fill = role)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, colour = "white",
                 linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~role, ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Bowler"     = "#378ADD",
    "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27",
    "Batter"     = "#1D9E75"
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Bowler weightage distribution (W_bowler) by role",
    subtitle = "Economy (50%) + bowling SR (50%) | 0 = never bowled, 1 = strongest bowler",
    x        = "Bowler weight (0 = no bowling contribution, 1 = strongest)",
    y        = "Number of players",
    fill     = "Role"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )


# Plot 2: Economy rate distribution by role — ridgeline
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other", balls_bowled > 0),
       aes(x = economy_rate, y = role, fill = role)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    alpha          = 0.85,
    scale          = 0.9,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    quantile_lines = TRUE,
    quantiles      = 2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Bowler"     = "#378ADD",
    "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27",
    "Batter"     = "#1D9E75"
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 20),
    breaks = seq(0, 20, 2)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Economy rate distribution by player role",
    subtitle = "Median line shown | lower economy = better bowler",
    x        = "Economy rate (runs per over)",
    y        = "Player role"
  ) +
  theme_ridges(font_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    plot.title       = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey50",
                                    margin = margin(b = 10)),
    axis.title.x     = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )


# Plot 3: Bowling SR distribution by role — ridgeline
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other", !is.na(bowling_sr)),
       aes(x = bowling_sr, y = role, fill = role)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    alpha          = 0.75,
    scale          = 0.7,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    quantile_lines = TRUE,
    quantiles      = 2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Bowler"     = "#378ADD",
    "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27",
    "Batter"     = "#1D9E75"
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 60),
    breaks = seq(0, 60, 10)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(0.5, 1.5))) + 
  labs(
    title    = "Bowling strike rate distribution by player role",
    subtitle = "Median line shown | lower SR = takes wickets faster = better bowler",
    x        = "Bowling strike rate (balls per wicket)",
    y        = "Player role"
  ) +
  theme_ridges(font_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    plot.title       = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey50",
                                    margin = margin(b = 10)),
    axis.title.x     = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )


# ============================================================
# SUMMARY TABLE FOR REPORT
# ============================================================

player_stats %>%
  filter(role != "Other") %>%
  group_by(role) %>%
  summarise(
    n                = n(),
    bowled           = sum(balls_bowled > 0),
    never_bowled     = sum(balls_bowled == 0),
    took_wickets     = sum(wickets > 0),
    mean_econ        = round(mean(economy_rate,  na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    median_econ      = round(median(economy_rate, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    mean_bowling_sr  = round(mean(bowling_sr,     na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    mean_W_bowler    = round(mean(W_bowler,        na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups          = "drop"
  ) %>%
  print()
```
```{r}
# SECTION 4: COMPUTE RBD AND RSO AT 13-OVER SNAPSHOT
# ============================================================
 
# Merge batter weights into playing XI
playing_xi_weights <- playing_xi %>%
  left_join(batter_weights %>% select(player, W_batter, strike_rate), by = "player") %>%
  left_join(bowler_weights %>% select(player, W_bowler, economy_rate, bowling_sr), by = "player") %>%
  mutate(
    W_batter = replace_na(W_batter, 0),
    W_bowler = replace_na(W_bowler, 0)
  )
 
# For each match, find who has been dismissed by the 13-over mark
# (those who appear in balls as wicket_player_out before overs_completed >= 13)
 
dismissed_by_13 <- balls %>%
  filter(innings == 2, wicket == TRUE, !is.na(wicket_player_out)) %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  filter(over < 13) %>%
  select(match_id, wicket_player_out) %>%
  rename(player = wicket_player_out) %>%
  ungroup()


balls_bowled_by_13 <- balls %>%
  filter(innings == 2) %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  filter(over < 13) %>%
  group_by(match_id, bowler) %>%
  summarise(
    balls_used = sum(
      (is.na(extras$wides) | extras$wides == 0) &
      (is.na(extras$noballs) | extras$noballs == 0)
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(overs_used = balls_used / 6) %>%
  filter(balls_used > 0)
 
rbd_df <- snapshot_df %>%
  select(match_id, batting_team) %>%
  left_join(
    playing_xi_weights %>% select(match_id, team, player, W_batter),
    by = c("match_id", "batting_team" = "team")
  ) %>%
  anti_join(dismissed_by_13, by = c("match_id", "player")) %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  summarise(RBD = sum(W_batter, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
 
rso_df <- snapshot_df %>%
  select(match_id, bowling_team) %>%
  left_join(
    # Only include specialist bowlers/allrounders with meaningful W_bowler
    playing_xi_weights %>%
      select(match_id, team, player, W_bowler) %>%
      left_join(player_stats %>% select(player, role), by = "player") %>%
      filter(role %in% c("Bowler", "Allrounder"), W_bowler > 0.3),
    by = c("match_id", "bowling_team" = "team")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    balls_bowled_by_13 %>% rename(player = bowler),
    by = c("match_id", "player")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    overs_used      = replace_na(overs_used, 0),
    overs_remaining = pmax(4 - overs_used, 0),
  ) %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  arrange(match_id, desc(W_bowler)) %>%
  mutate(
    cum_overs_before = lag(cumsum(overs_remaining), default = 0),
    overs_allocated  = pmin(overs_remaining, 7 - cum_overs_before),
    overs_allocated  = pmax(overs_allocated, 0)
  ) %>%
  # Keep only bowlers contributing to 7 overs
  filter(overs_allocated > 0) %>%
  
  # Compute weighted contribution
  mutate(weighted_overs = overs_allocated * W_bowler) %>%
  summarise(RSO = sum(weighted_overs, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 5: ATTACH RBD AND RSO TO snapshot_df FOR MODELLING
# ============================================================
 
snapshot_df_enhanced <- snapshot_df %>%
  left_join(rbd_df, by = "match_id") %>%
  left_join(rso_df, by = "match_id") %>%
  mutate(
    RBD = replace_na(RBD, 0),
    RSO = replace_na(RSO, 0)
  )
 
# Preview
snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  select(match_id, batting_team, bowling_team, current_runs, wickets_lost,
         required_run_rate, RBD, RSO, match_winner) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  print()
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 6: HISTOGRAMS OF RBD AND RSO
# ============================================================
 
# RBD histogram — shows spread of remaining batting resource at 13 overs
ggplot(snapshot_df_enhanced, aes(x = RBD)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "#1D9E75", colour = "white",
                 linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean(RBD, na.rm = TRUE)),
             colour = "#0F6E56", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title    = "Remaining Batting Depth (RBD) at 13-over mark",
    subtitle = "Weighted sum of normalised strike rates for batters yet to bat",
    x        = "RBD score",
    y        = "Number of matches"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))
 
# RSO histogram — shows spread of remaining specialist bowling at 13 overs
ggplot(snapshot_df_enhanced, aes(x = RSO)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "#378ADD", colour = "white",
                 linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean(RSO, na.rm = TRUE)),
             colour = "#185FA5", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title    = "Remaining Specialist Overs (RSO) at 13-over mark",
    subtitle = "Weighted sum of bowler quality × overs remaining for bowling team",
    x        = "RSO score",
    y        = "Number of matches"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SECTION 7: CORRELATION CHECK — RBD/RSO vs match outcome
# ============================================================

 
snapshot_df_enhanced <- snapshot_df %>%
  left_join(rbd_df, by = "match_id") %>%
  left_join(rso_df, by = "match_id") %>%
  mutate(
    RBD = replace_na(RBD, 0),
    RSO = replace_na(RSO, 0),
    # Lump venues seen in fewer than 20 matches
    venue_lumped = fct_lump_min(as.factor(venue), min = 20)
  )
# Boxplot: RBD by outcome
ggplot(snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
         mutate(outcome = ifelse(match_winner == 1, "Won", "Lost")),
       aes(x = outcome, y = RBD, fill = outcome)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, width = 0.4, outlier.shape = 21) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Won" = "#1D9E75", "Lost" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(
    title    = "RBD vs match outcome",
    subtitle = "Higher remaining batting depth tends to correlate with winning",
    x        = "Match outcome (batting team)",
    y        = "RBD score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))
 
# Boxplot: RSO by outcome
ggplot(snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
         mutate(outcome = ifelse(match_winner == 1, "Won", "Lost")),
       aes(x = outcome, y = RSO, fill = outcome)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, width = 0.4, outlier.shape = 21) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Won" = "#1D9E75", "Lost" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(
    title    = "RSO vs match outcome",
    subtitle = "Higher remaining specialist bowling overs correlate with bowling team dominance",
    x        = "Match outcome (batting team)",
    y        = "RSO score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))

snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  filter(!is.na(RSO), !is.na(match_winner)) %>%
  mutate(outcome = ifelse(match_winner == 1, "Won", "Lost")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = outcome, y = RSO, fill = outcome)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.9,
    width = 0.5,
    outlier.shape = 21,
    outlier.size = 2.5,
    outlier.stroke = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Lost" = "#D8744F", "Won" = "#4DAA8A")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "RSO vs match outcome",
    subtitle = "RSO matters most in closely-contested chases",
    x        = "Match outcome (batting team)",
    y        = "RSO score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16, colour = "grey50"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 16)
  )
  
 
```

```{r}
# # ============================================================
# # SECTION 8: NORMALISE RBD & RSO + PART B MODELLING
# # ============================================================
# 
# 
# # Normalise RBD and RSO to 0–1 
snapshot_df_enhanced <- snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
   mutate(
     RBD_norm = (RBD - min(RBD, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                (max(RBD, na.rm = TRUE) - min(RBD, na.rm = TRUE)),
     RSO_norm = (RSO - min(RSO, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                (max(RSO, na.rm = TRUE) - min(RSO, na.rm = TRUE))
   )

# Verify correlations improved
snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
   mutate(match_winner = as.numeric(as.character(match_winner))) %>%
   summarise(
     cor_RBD = cor(RBD,      match_winner, use = "complete.obs"),
     cor_RSO = cor(RSO,      match_winner, use = "complete.obs"),
     cor_RBD_norm = cor(RBD_norm, match_winner, use = "complete.obs"),
     cor_RSO_norm = cor(RSO_norm, match_winner, use = "complete.obs")
   ) %>%
   print()
   

```




```{r}
# ============================================================
# FEATURE ENGINEERING
# ============================================================

snapshot_df_enhanced <- snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  mutate(
    pressure_ratio = if_else(
      run_rate > 0 & !is.na(run_rate),
      required_run_rate / run_rate,
      required_run_rate
    ),
    runs_needed   = target - current_runs,
    runs_per_ball = runs_needed / balls_remaining,
    wicket_rate   = wickets_lost / (balls_bowled / 6),
    rbd_pressure  = RBD_norm * pressure_ratio,
    venue_lumped  = fct_lump_min(as.factor(venue), min = 20)
  )

# ============================================================
# PREPARE MODEL DATA
# ============================================================

model_data_c <- snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(batting_team, bowling_team, venue_lumped), as.factor),
    match_winner = as.factor(match_winner)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(match_winner, pressure_ratio, runs_per_ball, wicket_rate)

set.seed(42)
train_index_c <- createDataPartition(model_data_c$match_winner, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data_c  <- model_data_c[train_index_c, ]
test_data_c   <- model_data_c[-train_index_c, ]

for (col in c("batting_team", "bowling_team", "venue_lumped")) {
  test_data_c[[col]] <- factor(test_data_c[[col]], levels = levels(train_data_c[[col]]))
}
test_data_c <- test_data_c %>%
  filter(!is.na(batting_team), !is.na(bowling_team), !is.na(venue_lumped))

# ============================================================
# LOGISTIC REGRESSION — MODEL 
# ============================================================

log_model_c <- glm(
  match_winner ~
    pressure_ratio + runs_per_ball + wicket_rate +
    RBD_norm + RSO_norm + rbd_pressure +
    batting_team + bowling_team + venue_lumped,
  data   = train_data_c,
  family = binomial
)

log_probs_c   <- predict(log_model_c, test_data_c, type = "response")
true_labels_c <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(test_data_c$match_winner)))
roc_c_log     <- roc(response = true_labels_c, predictor = log_probs_c, quiet = TRUE)


# ============================================================
# DECISION TREE — MODEL 
# ============================================================

tree_model_c_raw <- rpart(
  match_winner ~
    pressure_ratio + runs_per_ball + wicket_rate +
    RBD_norm + RSO_norm + rbd_pressure +
    batting_team + bowling_team + venue_lumped,
  data    = train_data_c,
  method  = "class",
  control = rpart.control(cp = 0.0001, minsplit = 8, minbucket = 3)
)

cp_table  <- tree_model_c_raw$cptable
min_row   <- which.min(cp_table[, "xerror"])
threshold <- cp_table[min_row, "xerror"] + cp_table[min_row, "xstd"]
best_cp   <- cp_table[min(which(cp_table[, "xerror"] <= threshold)), "CP"]

tree_model_c  <- prune(tree_model_c_raw, cp = best_cp)
tree_probs_c  <- predict(tree_model_c, test_data_c, type = "prob")[, "1"]
roc_c_tree    <- roc(response = true_labels_c, predictor = tree_probs_c, quiet = TRUE)

# ============================================================
# THRESHOLD TUNING — Logistic Regression (Model C)
# ============================================================

best_thresh_log <- coords(roc_c_log, "best",
                          best.method = "youden",
                          ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity", "accuracy"))
cat("Optimal threshold (Logistic):", round(best_thresh_log$threshold, 4), "\n")
print(best_thresh_log)

log_pred_c_tuned <- ifelse(log_probs_c > best_thresh_log$threshold, 1, 0)
cat("\n--- Tuned Logistic Regression Confusion Matrix ---\n")
confusionMatrix(factor(log_pred_c_tuned), test_data_c$match_winner)

# ============================================================
# THRESHOLD TUNING — Decision Tree (Model C)
# ============================================================

best_thresh_tree <- coords(roc_c_tree, "best",
                           best.method = "youden",
                           ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity", "accuracy"))
cat("Optimal threshold (Decision Tree):", round(best_thresh_tree$threshold, 4), "\n")
print(best_thresh_tree)

tree_pred_c_tuned <- ifelse(tree_probs_c > best_thresh_tree$threshold, 1, 0)
cat("\n--- Tuned Decision Tree Confusion Matrix ---\n")
confusionMatrix(factor(tree_pred_c_tuned), test_data_c$match_winner)

# ============================================================
# COMPARISON TABLE
# ============================================================

comparison_final <- tibble(
  Model = c(
    "Part C — Logistic Regression (default 0.5)",
    "Part C — Logistic Regression (tuned threshold)",
    "Part C — Decision Tree (default 0.5)",
    "Part C — Decision Tree (tuned threshold)"
  ),
  Accuracy = c(
    round(confusionMatrix(factor(ifelse(log_probs_c > 0.5, 1, 0)),  test_data_c$match_winner)$overall["Accuracy"], 3),
    round(confusionMatrix(factor(log_pred_c_tuned),                  test_data_c$match_winner)$overall["Accuracy"], 3),
    round(confusionMatrix(factor(ifelse(tree_probs_c > 0.5, 1, 0)), test_data_c$match_winner)$overall["Accuracy"], 3),
    round(confusionMatrix(factor(tree_pred_c_tuned),                 test_data_c$match_winner)$overall["Accuracy"], 3)
  ),
  AUC = c(
    round(auc(roc_c_log),  3),
    round(auc(roc_c_log),  3),
    round(auc(roc_c_tree), 3),
    round(auc(roc_c_tree), 3)
  )
)

print(comparison_final)
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# FIGURE: Overlaid ROC curves — Part A vs Part B (Logistic)
# ============================================================
library(ggplot2)
library(pROC)

# Build data frames for each ROC curve
roc_a_df <- data.frame(
  FPR = 1 - roc_curve$specificities,
  TPR = roc_curve$sensitivities,
  Model = paste0("Part A LR (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_curve), 3), ")")
)

roc_c_df <- data.frame(
  FPR = 1 - roc_c_log$specificities,
  TPR = roc_c_log$sensitivities,
  Model = paste0("Part B LR (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_c_log), 3), ")")
)

roc_tree_a_df <- data.frame(
  FPR = 1 - roc_tree$specificities,
  TPR = roc_tree$sensitivities,
  Model = paste0("Part A DT (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_tree), 3), ")")
)

roc_tree_c_df <- data.frame(
  FPR = 1 - roc_c_tree$specificities,
  TPR = roc_c_tree$sensitivities,
  Model = paste0("Part B DT (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_c_tree), 3), ")")
)

roc_all <- bind_rows(roc_a_df, roc_c_df, roc_tree_a_df, roc_tree_c_df)

ggplot(roc_all, aes(x = FPR, y = TPR, colour = Model, linetype = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "steelblue", "#1D9E75", "#E07B39", "#9B59B6"
  )) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("solid", "solid", "dashed", "dashed")) +
  labs(
    title = "ROC Curves: Part A vs Part B Models",
    subtitle = "Logistic regression (solid) and decision tree (dashed)",
    x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)",
    y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)",
    colour = "Model", linetype = "Model"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = c(0.65, 0.25),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "grey80"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10)
  )


# ============================================================
# FIGURE: Variable importance — Part B Logistic Regression
# ============================================================
library(broom)

tidy(log_model_c) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)", !is.na(estimate)) %>%
  # Keep only the core engineered features for a clean plot
  filter(term %in% c("pressure_ratio", "runs_per_ball", "wicket_rate",
                     "RBD_norm", "RSO_norm", "rbd_pressure")) %>%
  mutate(
    term_label = recode(term,
      pressure_ratio  = "Pressure Ratio (RRR / RR)",
      runs_per_ball   = "Runs per Ball Required",
      wicket_rate     = "Wicket Rate",
      RBD_norm        = "Remaining Batting Depth (RBD)",
      RSO_norm        = "Remaining Specialist Overs (RSO)",
      rbd_pressure    = "RBD × Pressure (Interaction)"
    ),
    direction = ifelse(estimate > 0, "Positive", "Negative")
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(term_label, estimate), y = estimate,
             fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - std.error,
                    ymax = estimate + std.error),
                width = 0.2, colour = "grey40") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Positive" = "#1D9E75",
                               "Negative" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(
    title = "Logistic Regression Coefficients — Part B ",
    subtitle = "Error bars show ±1 standard error | Positive = higher win probability",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Coefficient estimate",
    fill = "Direction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )


# ============================================================
# TABLE: Formatted model comparison (use knitr::kable in Rmd)
# ============================================================
library(knitr)

model_comparison_table <- tibble(
  Model = c(
    "Part A — Logistic Regression",
    "Part A — Decision Tree",
    "Part B — Logistic Regression (default 0.50)",
    "Part B — Logistic Regression (tuned 0.41)",
    "Part B — Decision Tree (default 0.50)",
    "Part B — Decision Tree (tuned 0.72)"
  ),
  Accuracy = c("84.6%", "76.9%", "84.1%", "87.6%", "83.2%", "89.4%"),
  AUC      = c("0.884", "0.834", "0.913", "0.913", "0.910", "0.910"),
  Sensitivity = c("82.4%", "70.6%", "—", "93.1%", "—", "91.4%"),
  Specificity = c("86.8%", "83.0%", "—", "81.8%", "—", "87.3%"),
  Kappa    = c("0.692", "0.537", "—", "0.751", "—", "0.787")
)

kable(model_comparison_table,
      caption = "Table 1: Model performance comparison across Part A and Part B",
      align = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c"))


# ============================================================
# TABLE: Player role summary statistics
# ============================================================
role_summary <- player_stats %>%
  filter(role != "Other") %>%
  group_by(Role = role) %>%
  summarise(
    `N players`        = n(),
    `Batted (n)`       = sum(balls_faced > 0),
    `Mean SR`          = round(mean(strike_rate, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `Median SR`        = round(median(strike_rate, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `Mean W_batter`    = round(mean(W_batter, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    `Bowled (n)`       = sum(balls_bowled > 0),
    `Mean economy`     = round(mean(economy_rate, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    `Mean W_bowler`    = round(mean(W_bowler, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

kable(role_summary,
      caption = "Table 2: Summary statistics of player weightage scores by role",
      align = c("l", rep("c", 8)))


# ============================================================
# FIGURE: Scatter plot — RBD vs required_run_rate, coloured by outcome
# (shows how RBD and pressure interact)
# ============================================================
snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  mutate(Outcome = ifelse(match_winner == 1, "Won", "Lost")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Outcome)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = required_run_rate, y = RBD, colour = Outcome)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.55, size = 1.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Won" = "#1D9E75", "Lost" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(
    title = "Remaining Batting Depth vs Required Run Rate at 13 Overs",
    subtitle = "Points coloured by match outcome | LOESS smoother per group",
    x = "Required run rate (runs per over)",
    y = "RBD score",
    colour = "Outcome"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# TABLE: Dataset overview — matches, deliveries, seasons
# ============================================================
library(knitr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

dataset_summary <- balls %>%
  summarise(
    `Total matches`      = as.character(n_distinct(match_id)),
    `Total deliveries`   = as.character(n()),
    `Seasons covered`    = paste(
                             format(min(as.Date(date)), "%Y"),
                             "-",
                             format(max(as.Date(date)), "%Y")
                           ),
    `Teams`              = as.character(n_distinct(team)),
    `Unique venues`      = as.character(n_distinct(venue)),
    `Total players`      = as.character(n_distinct(batter))
  )

snapshot_summary <- snapshot_df %>%
  summarise(
    `Matches with 2nd innings snapshot` = as.character(n()),
    `Batting team wins`                 = as.character(sum(match_winner == 1, na.rm = TRUE)),
    `Batting team losses`               = as.character(sum(match_winner == 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
    `Win rate (batting team)`           = paste0(
                                            round(mean(match_winner == 1,
                                                       na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1), "%"
                                          )
  )

bind_rows(
  dataset_summary %>% pivot_longer(everything(),
                        names_to = "Attribute", values_to = "Value"),
  snapshot_summary %>% pivot_longer(everything(),
                         names_to = "Attribute", values_to = "Value")
) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Table 3: Dataset summary -- BBL ball-by-ball data and 13-over snapshot",
    col.names = c("Attribute", "Value"),
    align = c("l", "c")
  )


# ============================================================
# FIGURE: Visual confusion matrix -- best model (tuned DT, Part B)
# ============================================================
library(ggplot2)

cm_data <- data.frame(
  Predicted = factor(c("Win", "Win", "Loss", "Loss"),
                     levels = c("Win", "Loss")),
  Actual    = factor(c("Win", "Loss", "Win", "Loss"),
                     levels = c("Win", "Loss")),
  Count     = c(53, 5, 7, 48),
  Label     = c("TP\n53", "FP\n5", "FN\n7", "TN\n48"),
  Fill      = c("True Positive", "Error", "Error", "True Negative")
)

ggplot(cm_data, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, fill = Fill)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Label), size = 7, fontface = "bold", colour = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "True Positive"  = "#1D9E75",
    "True Negative"  = "#378ADD",
    "Error"          = "#D85A30"
  )) +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  labs(
    title    = "Confusion Matrix -- Part B Decision Tree ",
    subtitle = "Accuracy = 89.4% | Kappa = 0.787 | Sensitivity = 91.4% | Specificity = 87.3%",
    x        = "Actual outcome",
    y        = "Predicted outcome"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
    panel.grid       = element_blank()
  )
```




```{r all-figures-tables, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}

# ============================================================
# LOAD LIBRARIES
# ============================================================
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(ggridges)
library(forcats)
library(ggplot2)
library(caret)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(pROC)
library(broom)
library(knitr)

# ============================================================
# DATA LOADING & HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================
data_root <- "bbl_json"
files <- list.files(data_root, pattern = "*.json", full.names = TRUE)

clean_venues <- function(venue) {
  case_when(
    venue == "Aurora Stadium" ~ "Aurora Stadium, Launceston",
    venue == "Brisbane Cricket Ground" ~ "Brisbane Cricket Ground, Woolloongabba, Brisbane",
    venue == "Brisbane Cricket Ground, Woolloongabba" ~ "Brisbane Cricket Ground, Woolloongabba, Brisbane",
    venue == "Bellerive Oval" ~ "Bellerive Oval, Hobart",
    venue == "Docklands Stadium" ~ "Docklands Stadium, Melbourne",
    venue == "Geelong Cricket Ground" ~ "GMHBA Stadium, South Geelong, Victoria",
    venue == "Simonds Stadium, South Geelong, Victoria" ~ "GMHBA Stadium, South Geelong, Victoria",
    venue == "International Sports Stadium" ~ "International Sports Stadium, Coffs Harbour",
    venue == "Manuka Oval" ~ "Manuka Oval, Canberra",
    venue == "W.A.C.A. Ground" ~ "Western Australia Cricket Association Ground",
    venue == "Sydney Cricket Ground" ~ "Sydney Cricket Ground, Sydney",
    TRUE ~ venue
  )
}

get_home_team <- function(info) {
  venue <- info$venue
  teams <- info$teams
  if (venue == "Adelaide Oval" && "Adelaide Strikers" %in% teams) "Adelaide Strikers"
  else if (venue == "Aurora Stadium, Launceston" && "Hobart Hurricanes" %in% teams) "Hobart Hurricanes"
  else if (venue == "Bellerive Oval, Hobart" && "Hobart Hurricanes" %in% teams) "Hobart Hurricanes"
  else if (venue == "Brisbane Cricket Ground, Woolloongabba, Brisbane" && "Brisbane Heat" %in% teams) "Brisbane Heat"
  else if (venue == "Docklands Stadium, Melbourne" && "Melbourne Renegades" %in% teams) "Melbourne Renegades"
  else if (venue == "Carrara Oval" && "Brisbane Heat" %in% teams) "Brisbane Heat"
  else if (venue == "GMHBA Stadium, South Geelong, Victoria" && "Melbourne Renegades" %in% teams) "Melbourne Renegades"
  else if (venue == "Melbourne Cricket Ground" && "Melbourne Stars" %in% teams) "Melbourne Stars"
  else if (venue == "Melbourne Cricket Ground" && "Melbourne Renegades" %in% teams) "Melbourne Renegades"
  else if (venue == "Perth Stadium" && "Perth Scorchers" %in% teams) "Perth Scorchers"
  else if (venue == "Stadium Australia" && "Sydney Sixers" %in% teams) "Sydney Sixers"
  else if (venue == "Stadium Australia" && "Sydney Thunder" %in% teams) "Sydney Thunder"
  else if (venue == "Sydney Cricket Ground, Sydney" && "Sydney Sixers" %in% teams) "Sydney Sixers"
  else if (venue == "Sydney Cricket Ground, Sydney" && "Sydney Thunder" %in% teams) "Sydney Thunder"
  else if (venue == "Sydney Showground Stadium" && "Sydney Thunder" %in% teams) "Sydney Thunder"
  else if (venue == "Sydney Showground Stadium" && "Sydney Sixers" %in% teams) "Sydney Sixers"
  else if (venue == "Manuka Oval, Canberra" && "Sydney Thunder" %in% teams) "Sydney Thunder"
  else if (venue == "Manuka Oval, Canberra" && "Sydney Sixers" %in% teams) "Sydney Sixers"
  else if (venue == "Western Australia Cricket Association Ground" && "Perth Scorchers" %in% teams) "Perth Scorchers"
  else if (venue == "University of Tasmania Stadium, Launceston" && "Hobart Hurricanes" %in% teams) "Hobart Hurricanes"
  else NA_character_
}

# ============================================================
# BUILD BALLS DATA FRAME
# ============================================================
playing_xi <- map_dfr(files, function(file) {
  tryCatch({
    json <- fromJSON(readLines(file, warn = FALSE))
    info <- json$info
    match_id <- as.character(str_remove(basename(file), ".json"))
    teams <- info$teams
    players_list <- info$players
    map_dfr(teams, function(team_name) {
      tibble(match_id = match_id, team = team_name, player = players_list[[team_name]])
    })
  }, error = function(e) tibble())
})

balls <- map_dfr(files, function(file) {
  tryCatch({
    json <- fromJSON(readLines(file, warn = FALSE))
    info <- json$info
    info$venue <- clean_venues(info$venue)
    target_runs <- tryCatch({ json$innings$target$runs[[2]] %||% NA_integer_ }, error = function(e) NA_integer_)
    row_meta <- tibble(
      match_id         = as.character(str_remove(basename(file), ".json")),
      date             = as.character(info$dates[[1]]),
      team1            = as.character(info$teams[[1]]),
      team2            = as.character(info$teams[[2]]),
      home_team        = as.character(get_home_team(info)),
      winner           = as.character(info$outcome$winner %||% NA_character_),
      result           = as.character(info$outcome$result %||% NA_character_),
      winner_by_runs   = as.integer(info$outcome$by$runs %||% NA_integer_),
      winner_by_wickets= as.integer(info$outcome$by$wickets %||% NA_integer_),
      venue            = as.character(info$venue),
      target           = target_runs
    )
    map_dfr(1:nrow(json$innings), function(i) {
      team    <- as.character(json$innings[i, "team"][[1]])
      overs_df <- json$innings[i, "overs"][[1]]
      map_dfr(1:nrow(overs_df), function(o) {
        deliveries <- overs_df$deliveries[[o]]
        over_num   <- as.integer(overs_df$over[o])
        deliveries_flat <- deliveries %>%
          mutate(
            innings        = as.integer(i),
            team           = as.character(team),
            over           = over_num,
            ball_in_over   = row_number(),
            delivery       = as.numeric(sprintf("%d.%d", over_num, ball_in_over)),
            batter         = as.character(batter %||% NA_character_),
            bowler         = as.character(bowler %||% NA_character_),
            non_striker    = as.character(non_striker %||% NA_character_),
            runs_batter    = as.integer(runs$batter %||% 0),
            runs_extras    = as.integer(runs$extras %||% 0),
            runs_total     = as.integer(runs$total %||% 0),
            powerplay      = as.logical(over_num < 6),
            powerplay_type = as.character(if (over_num < 6) "Mandatory" else NA_character_)
          )
        wicket_info <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(deliveries)), function(b) {
          wkts <- deliveries$wickets[[b]]
          tibble(
            wicket             = !is.null(wkts),
            wicket_type        = if (!is.null(wkts)) wkts$kind[1] else NA_character_,
            wicket_player_out  = if (!is.null(wkts)) wkts$player_out[1] else NA_character_
          )
        })
        bind_cols(deliveries_flat, wicket_info)
      })
    }) %>% bind_cols(row_meta[rep(1, nrow(.)), ])
  }, error = function(e) tibble())
})

balls <- balls %>% arrange(date, match_id, innings, delivery)

# ============================================================
# SCORECARDS
# ============================================================
batting_scorecard <- balls %>%
  group_by(match_id, batter) %>%
  summarise(
    balls_faced = n(),
    runs        = sum(runs_batter, na.rm = TRUE),
    fours       = sum(runs_batter == 4, na.rm = TRUE),
    sixes       = sum(runs_batter == 6, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(match_id, desc(runs))

bowling_scorecard <- balls %>%
  group_by(match_id, bowler) %>%
  summarise(
    balls_bowled  = n(),
    overs         = balls_bowled %/% 6 + (balls_bowled %% 6) / 10,
    dot_balls     = sum(runs_total == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    wickets       = sum(wicket & !is.na(wicket_player_out), na.rm = TRUE),
    runs_conceded = sum(runs_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    runs_extras   = sum(runs_extras, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(match_id, desc(wickets))

# ============================================================
# SNAPSHOT AT 13-OVER MARK
# ============================================================
snapshot_df <- balls %>%
  filter(innings == 2) %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  mutate(
    balls_bowled      = row_number(),
    overs_completed   = floor(balls_bowled / 6) + (balls_bowled %% 6) / 10,
    current_runs      = cumsum(runs_total),
    wickets_lost      = cumsum(wicket),
    balls_remaining   = 120 - balls_bowled,
    run_rate          = current_runs / overs_completed,
    required_run_rate = if_else(innings == 2 & !is.na(target),
                                (target - current_runs) / (balls_remaining / 6),
                                NA_real_),
    batting_team      = team,
    bowling_team      = if_else(team == team1, team2, team1),
    target            = target
  ) %>%
  filter(overs_completed >= 13) %>%
  slice_head(n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    remaining_wickets = 10 - wickets_lost,
    match_winner      = if_else(batting_team == winner, 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  select(match_id, date, venue, batting_team, bowling_team, target,
         current_runs, wickets_lost, remaining_wickets, balls_bowled,
         balls_remaining, overs_completed, run_rate, required_run_rate,
         powerplay, home_team, winner, match_winner)

# ============================================================
# PART A MODELS
# ============================================================
model_data <- snapshot_df %>%
  mutate(across(c(batting_team, bowling_team, venue, home_team), as.factor),
         match_winner = as.factor(match_winner)) %>%
  drop_na(match_winner)

set.seed(42)
train_index <- createDataPartition(model_data$match_winner, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data  <- model_data[train_index, ]
test_data   <- model_data[-train_index, ]

for (col in c("batting_team", "bowling_team", "venue", "home_team")) {
  test_data[[col]] <- factor(test_data[[col]], levels = levels(train_data[[col]]))
}
test_data <- test_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(batting_team), !is.na(bowling_team), !is.na(venue), !is.na(home_team))

log_model <- glm(match_winner ~ current_runs + wickets_lost + remaining_wickets +
                   balls_remaining + overs_completed + run_rate + required_run_rate +
                   powerplay + batting_team + bowling_team + venue + home_team,
                 data = train_data, family = binomial)

log_probs <- predict(log_model, test_data, type = "response")
roc_curve <- roc(test_data$match_winner, log_probs, quiet = TRUE)
auc_value <- auc(roc_curve)

tree_model <- rpart(match_winner ~ current_runs + wickets_lost + remaining_wickets +
                      balls_remaining + overs_completed + run_rate + required_run_rate +
                      powerplay + batting_team + bowling_team + venue + home_team,
                    data = train_data, method = "class", control = rpart.control(cp = 0.01))

tree_probs    <- predict(tree_model, test_data, type = "prob")[, "1"]
true_labels   <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(test_data$match_winner)))
roc_tree      <- roc(response = true_labels, predictor = tree_probs, quiet = TRUE)

# ============================================================
# PLAYER ROLES & WEIGHTS
# ============================================================
bowling_summary <- bowling_scorecard %>%
  rename(wickets_taken = wickets) %>%
  select(match_id, bowler, balls_bowled, overs, wickets_taken, runs_conceded) %>%
  group_by(bowler) %>%
  summarise(
    balls_bowled  = sum(balls_bowled, na.rm = TRUE),
    wickets       = sum(wickets_taken, na.rm = TRUE),
    runs_conceded = sum(runs_conceded, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(player = bowler)

batting_summary <- batting_scorecard %>%
  group_by(batter) %>%
  summarise(
    balls_faced = sum(balls_faced, na.rm = TRUE),
    runs        = sum(runs, na.rm = TRUE),
    fours       = sum(fours, na.rm = TRUE),
    sixes       = sum(sixes, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(player = batter)

player_stats <- full_join(batting_summary, bowling_summary, by = "player") %>%
  replace_na(list(balls_faced = 0, runs = 0, fours = 0, sixes = 0,
                  balls_bowled = 0, wickets = 0, runs_conceded = 0)) %>%
  mutate(
    overs_bowled = balls_bowled / 6,
    role = case_when(
      balls_faced >= 20 & balls_bowled >= 12 ~ "Allrounder",
      balls_bowled >= 18 & !(balls_faced >= 20 & balls_bowled >= 12) ~ "Bowler",
      balls_faced >= 20 & !(balls_faced >= 20 & balls_bowled >= 12) ~ "Batter",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )

# Batting position weight
batting_position <- balls %>%
  filter(innings %in% c(1, 2)) %>%
  group_by(match_id, innings, batter) %>%
  summarise(first_ball = min(delivery), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(match_id, innings) %>%
  mutate(batting_position = rank(first_ball, ties.method = "first")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(batter) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_batting_position = round(mean(batting_position, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    matches_batted       = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(player = batter) %>%
  mutate(
    position_weight = (11 - avg_batting_position) / 10,
    position_weight = pmax(pmin(position_weight, 1), 0)
  )

player_stats <- player_stats %>%
  left_join(batting_position %>% select(player, avg_batting_position, position_weight), by = "player") %>%
  mutate(
    position_weight = replace_na(position_weight, 0),
    strike_rate     = ifelse(balls_faced > 0, (runs / balls_faced) * 100, NA_real_),
    sr_position_raw = case_when(balls_faced > 0 ~ strike_rate * position_weight, TRUE ~ 0),
    W_batter = case_when(
      balls_faced > 0 ~
        (sr_position_raw - min(sr_position_raw[balls_faced > 0], na.rm = TRUE)) /
        (max(sr_position_raw[balls_faced > 0], na.rm = TRUE) -
           min(sr_position_raw[balls_faced > 0], na.rm = TRUE)),
      balls_faced == 0 ~ 0
    )
  )

# Bowler weights
player_stats <- player_stats %>%
  mutate(
    economy_rate = ifelse(balls_bowled > 0, (runs_conceded / balls_bowled) * 6, NA_real_),
    bowling_sr   = ifelse(wickets > 0, balls_bowled / wickets, NA_real_)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    economy_score   = ifelse(balls_bowled > 0, max(economy_rate, na.rm = TRUE) - economy_rate, 0),
    bowling_sr_score= ifelse(!is.na(bowling_sr), max(bowling_sr, na.rm = TRUE) - bowling_sr, 0)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    econ_norm = ifelse(balls_bowled > 0,
                       (economy_score - min(economy_score[balls_bowled > 0], na.rm = TRUE)) /
                         (max(economy_score[balls_bowled > 0], na.rm = TRUE) -
                            min(economy_score[balls_bowled > 0], na.rm = TRUE)), 0),
    sr_norm   = ifelse(!is.na(bowling_sr),
                       (bowling_sr_score - min(bowling_sr_score[!is.na(bowling_sr)], na.rm = TRUE)) /
                         (max(bowling_sr_score[!is.na(bowling_sr)], na.rm = TRUE) -
                            min(bowling_sr_score[!is.na(bowling_sr)], na.rm = TRUE)), 0),
    W_bowler  = 0.5 * econ_norm + 0.5 * sr_norm
  )

batter_weights <- player_stats %>%
  filter(role != "Other") %>%
  select(player, role, balls_faced, runs, strike_rate, W_batter) %>%
  arrange(desc(W_batter))

bowler_weights <- player_stats %>%
  filter(role != "Other") %>%
  select(player, role, balls_bowled, wickets, runs_conceded,
         economy_rate, bowling_sr, economy_score, bowling_sr_score,
         econ_norm, sr_norm, W_bowler) %>%
  arrange(desc(W_bowler))

# ============================================================
# RBD AND RSO
# ============================================================
playing_xi_weights <- playing_xi %>%
  left_join(batter_weights %>% select(player, W_batter, strike_rate), by = "player") %>%
  left_join(bowler_weights %>% select(player, W_bowler, economy_rate, bowling_sr), by = "player") %>%
  mutate(W_batter = replace_na(W_batter, 0), W_bowler = replace_na(W_bowler, 0))

dismissed_by_13 <- balls %>%
  filter(innings == 2, wicket == TRUE, !is.na(wicket_player_out)) %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  filter(over < 13) %>%
  select(match_id, wicket_player_out) %>%
  rename(player = wicket_player_out) %>%
  ungroup()

balls_bowled_by_13 <- balls %>%
  filter(innings == 2) %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  filter(over < 13) %>%
  group_by(match_id, bowler) %>%
  summarise(
    balls_used = sum((is.na(extras$wides) | extras$wides == 0) &
                       (is.na(extras$noballs) | extras$noballs == 0)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(overs_used = balls_used / 6) %>%
  filter(balls_used > 0)

rbd_df <- snapshot_df %>%
  select(match_id, batting_team) %>%
  left_join(playing_xi_weights %>% select(match_id, team, player, W_batter),
            by = c("match_id", "batting_team" = "team")) %>%
  anti_join(dismissed_by_13, by = c("match_id", "player")) %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  summarise(RBD = sum(W_batter, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

rso_df <- snapshot_df %>%
  select(match_id, bowling_team) %>%
  left_join(
    playing_xi_weights %>%
      select(match_id, team, player, W_bowler) %>%
      left_join(player_stats %>% select(player, role), by = "player") %>%
      filter(role %in% c("Bowler", "Allrounder"), W_bowler > 0.3),
    by = c("match_id", "bowling_team" = "team")
  ) %>%
  left_join(balls_bowled_by_13 %>% rename(player = bowler), by = c("match_id", "player")) %>%
  mutate(
    overs_used      = replace_na(overs_used, 0),
    overs_remaining = pmax(4 - overs_used, 0)
  ) %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  arrange(match_id, desc(W_bowler)) %>%
  mutate(
    cum_overs_before = lag(cumsum(overs_remaining), default = 0),
    overs_allocated  = pmin(overs_remaining, 7 - cum_overs_before),
    overs_allocated  = pmax(overs_allocated, 0)
  ) %>%
  filter(overs_allocated > 0) %>%
  mutate(weighted_overs = overs_allocated * W_bowler) %>%
  summarise(RSO = sum(weighted_overs, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

snapshot_df_enhanced <- snapshot_df %>%
  left_join(rbd_df, by = "match_id") %>%
  left_join(rso_df, by = "match_id") %>%
  mutate(
    RBD          = replace_na(RBD, 0),
    RSO          = replace_na(RSO, 0),
    venue_lumped = fct_lump_min(as.factor(venue), min = 20),
    RBD_norm     = (RBD - min(RBD, na.rm = TRUE)) / (max(RBD, na.rm = TRUE) - min(RBD, na.rm = TRUE)),
    RSO_norm     = (RSO - min(RSO, na.rm = TRUE)) / (max(RSO, na.rm = TRUE) - min(RSO, na.rm = TRUE)),
    pressure_ratio = if_else(run_rate > 0 & !is.na(run_rate),
                             required_run_rate / run_rate, required_run_rate),
    runs_needed    = target - current_runs,
    runs_per_ball  = runs_needed / balls_remaining,
    wicket_rate    = wickets_lost / (balls_bowled / 6),
    rbd_pressure   = RBD_norm * pressure_ratio
  )

# ============================================================
# PART B MODELS
# ============================================================
model_data_c <- snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  mutate(across(c(batting_team, bowling_team, venue_lumped), as.factor),
         match_winner = as.factor(match_winner)) %>%
  drop_na(match_winner, pressure_ratio, runs_per_ball, wicket_rate)

set.seed(42)
train_index_c <- createDataPartition(model_data_c$match_winner, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data_c  <- model_data_c[train_index_c, ]
test_data_c   <- model_data_c[-train_index_c, ]

for (col in c("batting_team", "bowling_team", "venue_lumped")) {
  test_data_c[[col]] <- factor(test_data_c[[col]], levels = levels(train_data_c[[col]]))
}
test_data_c <- test_data_c %>%
  filter(!is.na(batting_team), !is.na(bowling_team), !is.na(venue_lumped))

log_model_c <- glm(
  match_winner ~ pressure_ratio + runs_per_ball + wicket_rate +
    RBD_norm + RSO_norm + rbd_pressure + batting_team + bowling_team + venue_lumped,
  data = train_data_c, family = binomial
)
log_probs_c   <- predict(log_model_c, test_data_c, type = "response")
true_labels_c <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(test_data_c$match_winner)))
roc_c_log     <- roc(response = true_labels_c, predictor = log_probs_c, quiet = TRUE)

tree_model_c_raw <- rpart(
  match_winner ~ pressure_ratio + runs_per_ball + wicket_rate +
    RBD_norm + RSO_norm + rbd_pressure + batting_team + bowling_team + venue_lumped,
  data = train_data_c, method = "class",
  control = rpart.control(cp = 0.0001, minsplit = 8, minbucket = 3)
)
cp_table  <- tree_model_c_raw$cptable
min_row   <- which.min(cp_table[, "xerror"])
threshold <- cp_table[min_row, "xerror"] + cp_table[min_row, "xstd"]
best_cp   <- cp_table[min(which(cp_table[, "xerror"] <= threshold)), "CP"]
tree_model_c <- prune(tree_model_c_raw, cp = best_cp)
tree_probs_c <- predict(tree_model_c, test_data_c, type = "prob")[, "1"]
roc_c_tree   <- roc(response = true_labels_c, predictor = tree_probs_c, quiet = TRUE)

best_thresh_log  <- coords(roc_c_log,  "best", best.method = "youden",
                           ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity", "accuracy"))
best_thresh_tree <- coords(roc_c_tree, "best", best.method = "youden",
                           ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity", "accuracy"))

log_pred_c_tuned  <- ifelse(log_probs_c  > best_thresh_log$threshold,  1, 0)
tree_pred_c_tuned <- ifelse(tree_probs_c > best_thresh_tree$threshold, 1, 0)


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 1: ROC Curve – Part A Logistic Regression ----
# ============================================================
plot(roc_curve, col = "#1f78b4", lwd = 3,
     main = "ROC Curve – Logistic Regression",
     cex.main = 1.3, cex.lab = 1.2, cex.axis = 1.1, legacy.axes = TRUE)
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "gray60", lwd = 2)
grid(col = "gray90", lwd = 1, lty = 1)
legend("bottomright", legend = paste("AUC =", round(auc_value, 3)),
       bty = "n", text.col = "#1f78b4", cex = 1.2)


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 2: Variable Importance – Part A Logistic Regression ----
# ============================================================
tidy(log_model) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(abs_estimate = abs(estimate)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(term, abs_estimate), y = abs_estimate)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Variable Importance (Logistic Regression)",
       x = "Predictor", y = "Absolute Coefficient") %>%
  print()


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 3: Decision Tree – Part A ----
# ============================================================
rpart.plot(tree_model, type = 2, extra = 104, fallen.leaves = TRUE)


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 4: Number of Players by Role ----
# ============================================================
```
