```{r all-figures-tables, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
role_counts <- player_stats %>% count(role) %>% arrange(desc(n))
ggplot(role_counts, aes(x = reorder(role, n), y = n, fill = role)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Number of Players by Role (based on thresholds)",
       x = "Role", y = "Players") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 5: Balls Faced by Role – Ridgeline ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other", balls_faced > 0),
       aes(x = balls_faced, y = role, fill = role)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.85, scale = 1.2, rel_min_height = 0.01,
                      quantile_lines = TRUE, quantiles = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Batter" = "#1D9E75", "Bowler" = "#378ADD", "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27")) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma, limits = c(0, 2500), breaks = seq(0, 1500, 250)) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of balls faced by player role",
       subtitle = "Median line shown | x-axis capped at 2,500 balls to improve readability",
       x = "Balls faced (career total)", y = "Player role") +
  theme_ridges(font_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title    = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey50", margin = margin(b = 10)),
        axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.y  = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 6: Balls Bowled by Role – Ridgeline ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other", balls_bowled > 0),
       aes(x = balls_bowled, y = role, fill = role)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.75, scale = 0.9, rel_min_height = 0.01,
                      quantile_lines = TRUE, quantiles = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Batter" = "#1D9E75", "Bowler" = "#378ADD", "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27")) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma, limits = c(0, 1500), breaks = seq(0, 1500, 250)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(0.5, 1.2))) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of balls bowled by player role",
       subtitle = "Median line shown | x-axis capped at 1,500 balls to improve readability",
       x = "Balls bowled (career total)", y = "Player role") +
  theme_ridges(font_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title    = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey50", margin = margin(b = 10)),
        axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.y  = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 7: W_batter Distribution by Role ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(batter_weights %>% filter(role != "Other"), aes(x = W_batter, fill = role)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~role, ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Batter" = "#1D9E75", "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27", "Bowler" = "#378ADD")) +
  labs(title = "Batter weightage distribution (W_batter) by role",
       subtitle = "Batters/Allrounders normalised by SR | Bowlers: 0 if never batted, SR-based if they have",
       x = "Batter weight (0 = no batting contribution, 1 = highest SR)",
       y = "Number of players", fill = "Role") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "none")


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 8: W_bowler Distribution by Role ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other"), aes(x = W_bowler, fill = role)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~role, ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Bowler" = "#378ADD", "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27", "Batter" = "#1D9E75")) +
  labs(title = "Bowler weightage distribution (W_bowler) by role",
       subtitle = "Economy (50%) + bowling SR (50%) | 0 = never bowled, 1 = strongest bowler",
       x = "Bowler weight (0 = no bowling contribution, 1 = strongest)",
       y = "Number of players", fill = "Role") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "none")


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 9: Economy Rate Distribution by Role – Ridgeline ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other", balls_bowled > 0),
       aes(x = economy_rate, y = role, fill = role)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.85, scale = 0.9, rel_min_height = 0.01,
                      quantile_lines = TRUE, quantiles = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Bowler" = "#378ADD", "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27", "Batter" = "#1D9E75")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 20), breaks = seq(0, 20, 2)) +
  labs(title = "Economy rate distribution by player role",
       subtitle = "Median line shown | lower economy = better bowler",
       x = "Economy rate (runs per over)", y = "Player role") +
  theme_ridges(font_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title    = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey50", margin = margin(b = 10)),
        axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.y  = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 10: Bowling Strike Rate Distribution by Role – Ridgeline ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other", !is.na(bowling_sr)),
       aes(x = bowling_sr, y = role, fill = role)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.75, scale = 0.7, rel_min_height = 0.01,
                      quantile_lines = TRUE, quantiles = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Bowler" = "#378ADD", "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27", "Batter" = "#1D9E75")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 60), breaks = seq(0, 60, 10)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(0.5, 1.5))) +
  labs(title = "Bowling strike rate distribution by player role",
       subtitle = "Median line shown | lower SR = takes wickets faster = better bowler",
       x = "Bowling strike rate (balls per wicket)", y = "Player role") +
  theme_ridges(font_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title    = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey50", margin = margin(b = 10)),
        axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.y  = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 11: RBD Histogram ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(snapshot_df_enhanced, aes(x = RBD)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "#1D9E75", colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean(RBD, na.rm = TRUE)),
             colour = "#0F6E56", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Remaining Batting Depth (RBD) at 13-over mark",
       subtitle = "Weighted sum of normalised strike rates for batters yet to bat",
       x = "RBD score", y = "Number of matches") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 12: RSO Histogram ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(snapshot_df_enhanced, aes(x = RSO)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "#378ADD", colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean(RSO, na.rm = TRUE)),
             colour = "#185FA5", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Remaining Specialist Overs (RSO) at 13-over mark",
       subtitle = "Weighted sum of bowler quality × overs remaining for bowling team",
       x = "RSO score", y = "Number of matches") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 13: RBD vs Match Outcome Boxplot ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(snapshot_df_enhanced %>% mutate(outcome = ifelse(match_winner == 1, "Won", "Lost")),
       aes(x = outcome, y = RBD, fill = outcome)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, width = 0.4, outlier.shape = 21) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Won" = "#1D9E75", "Lost" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(title = "RBD vs match outcome",
       subtitle = "Higher remaining batting depth tends to correlate with winning",
       x = "Match outcome (batting team)", y = "RBD score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 14: RSO vs Match Outcome Boxplot ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(snapshot_df_enhanced %>% mutate(outcome = ifelse(match_winner == 1, "Won", "Lost")),
       aes(x = outcome, y = RSO, fill = outcome)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, width = 0.4, outlier.shape = 21) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Won" = "#1D9E75", "Lost" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(title = "RSO vs match outcome",
       subtitle = "Higher remaining specialist bowling overs correlate with bowling team dominance",
       x = "Match outcome (batting team)", y = "RSO score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 15: Overlaid ROC Curves – Part A vs Part B ----
# ============================================================
roc_a_df <- data.frame(FPR = 1 - roc_curve$specificities,  TPR = roc_curve$sensitivities,
                       Model = paste0("Part A LR (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_curve), 3), ")"))
roc_c_df <- data.frame(FPR = 1 - roc_c_log$specificities,  TPR = roc_c_log$sensitivities,
                       Model = paste0("Part B LR (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_c_log), 3), ")"))
roc_tree_a_df <- data.frame(FPR = 1 - roc_tree$specificities, TPR = roc_tree$sensitivities,
                             Model = paste0("Part A DT (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_tree), 3), ")"))
roc_tree_c_df <- data.frame(FPR = 1 - roc_c_tree$specificities, TPR = roc_c_tree$sensitivities,
                             Model = paste0("Part B DT (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_c_tree), 3), ")"))
roc_all <- bind_rows(roc_a_df, roc_c_df, roc_tree_a_df, roc_tree_c_df)

ggplot(roc_all, aes(x = FPR, y = TPR, colour = Model, linetype = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue", "#1D9E75", "#E07B39", "#9B59B6")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("solid", "solid", "dashed", "dashed")) +
  labs(title = "ROC Curves: Part A vs Part B Models",
       subtitle = "Logistic regression (solid) and decision tree (dashed)",
       x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)", y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)",
       colour = "Model", linetype = "Model") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position.inside = c(0.65, 0.25),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "grey80"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 16: Logistic Regression Coefficients – Part B ----
# ============================================================
tidy(log_model_c) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)", !is.na(estimate)) %>%
  filter(term %in% c("pressure_ratio", "runs_per_ball", "wicket_rate",
                     "RBD_norm", "RSO_norm", "rbd_pressure")) %>%
  mutate(
    term_label = recode(term,
                        pressure_ratio = "Pressure Ratio (RRR / RR)",
                        runs_per_ball  = "Runs per Ball Required",
                        wicket_rate    = "Wicket Rate",
                        RBD_norm       = "Remaining Batting Depth (RBD)",
                        RSO_norm       = "Remaining Specialist Overs (RSO)",
                        rbd_pressure   = "RBD × Pressure (Interaction)"),
    direction = ifelse(estimate > 0, "Positive", "Negative")
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(term_label, estimate), y = estimate, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - std.error, ymax = estimate + std.error),
                width = 0.2, colour = "grey40") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Positive" = "#1D9E75", "Negative" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(title = "Logistic Regression Coefficients - Part B",
       subtitle = "Error bars show ±1 standard error | Positive = higher win probability",
       x = NULL, y = "Coefficient estimate", fill = "Direction") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10), legend.position = "top")


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 17: RBD vs Required Run Rate Scatter ----
# ============================================================
snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  mutate(Outcome = ifelse(match_winner == 1, "Won", "Lost")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Outcome)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = required_run_rate, y = RBD, colour = Outcome)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.55, size = 1.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Won" = "#1D9E75", "Lost" = "#D85A30")) +
  labs(title = "Remaining Batting Depth vs Required Run Rate at 13 Overs",
       subtitle = "Points coloured by match outcome | LOESS smoother per group",
       x = "Required run rate (runs per over)", y = "RBD score", colour = "Outcome") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10), legend.position = "top")


# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 18: Confusion Matrix – Part B Decision Tree (tuned) ----
# ============================================================
cm_data <- data.frame(
  Predicted = factor(c("Win", "Win", "Loss", "Loss"), levels = c("Win", "Loss")),
  Actual    = factor(c("Win", "Loss", "Win", "Loss"), levels = c("Win", "Loss")),
  Count     = c(53, 5, 7, 48),
  Label     = c("TP\n53", "FP\n5", "FN\n7", "TN\n48"),
  Fill      = c("True Positive", "Error", "Error", "True Negative")
)
ggplot(cm_data, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, fill = Fill)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Label), size = 7, fontface = "bold", colour = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("True Positive" = "#1D9E75", "True Negative" = "#378ADD",
                               "Error" = "#D85A30")) +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix -- Part B Decision Tree",
       subtitle = "Accuracy = 89.4% | Kappa = 0.787 | Sensitivity = 91.4% | Specificity = 87.3%",
       x = "Actual outcome", y = "Predicted outcome") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
        panel.grid = element_blank())


# ============================================================
# ---- TABLE 1: Model Performance Comparison ----
# ============================================================
model_comparison_table <- tibble(
  Model = c(
    "Part A — Logistic Regression",
    "Part A — Decision Tree",
    "Part B — Logistic Regression (default 0.50)",
    "Part B — Logistic Regression (tuned 0.41)",
    "Part B — Decision Tree (default 0.50)",
    "Part B — Decision Tree (tuned 0.72)"
  ),
  Accuracy    = c("84.6%", "76.9%", "84.1%", "87.6%", "83.2%", "89.4%"),
  AUC         = c("0.884", "0.834", "0.913", "0.913", "0.910", "0.910"),
  Sensitivity = c("82.4%", "70.6%", "—",     "93.1%", "—",     "91.4%"),
  Specificity = c("86.8%", "83.0%", "—",     "81.8%", "—",     "87.3%"),
  Kappa       = c("0.692", "0.537", "—",     "0.751", "—",     "0.787")
)
kable(model_comparison_table,
      caption = "Table 1: Model performance comparison across Part A and Part B",
      align   = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c"))


# ============================================================
# ---- TABLE 2: Player Role Summary Statistics ----
# ============================================================
role_summary <- player_stats %>%
  filter(role != "Other") %>%
  group_by(Role = role) %>%
  summarise(
    `N players`      = n(),
    `Batted (n)`     = sum(balls_faced > 0),
    `Mean SR`        = round(mean(strike_rate,    na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `Median SR`      = round(median(strike_rate,  na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    `Mean W_batter`  = round(mean(W_batter,       na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    `Bowled (n)`     = sum(balls_bowled > 0),
    `Mean economy`   = round(mean(economy_rate,   na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    `Mean W_bowler`  = round(mean(W_bowler,       na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
kable(role_summary,
      caption = "Table 2: Summary statistics of player weightage scores by role",
      align   = c("l", rep("c", 8)))


# ============================================================
# ---- TABLE 3: Dataset Overview ----
# ============================================================
dataset_summary <- balls %>%
  summarise(
    `Total matches`    = as.character(n_distinct(match_id)),
    `Total deliveries` = as.character(n()),
    `Seasons covered`  = paste(format(min(as.Date(date)), "%Y"), "-",
                               format(max(as.Date(date)), "%Y")),
    `Teams`            = as.character(n_distinct(team)),
    `Unique venues`    = as.character(n_distinct(venue)),
    `Total players`    = as.character(n_distinct(batter))
  )

snapshot_summary <- snapshot_df %>%
  summarise(
    `Matches with 2nd innings snapshot` = as.character(n()),
    `Batting team wins`                 = as.character(sum(match_winner == 1, na.rm = TRUE)),
    `Batting team losses`               = as.character(sum(match_winner == 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
    `Win rate (batting team)`           = paste0(round(mean(match_winner == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1), "%")
  )

bind_rows(
  dataset_summary  %>% pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Attribute", values_to = "Value"),
  snapshot_summary %>% pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Attribute", values_to = "Value")
) %>%
  kable(caption = "Table 3: Dataset summary -- BBL ball-by-ball data and 13-over snapshot",
        col.names = c("Attribute", "Value"), align = c("l", "c"))

```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(DiagrammeR)

grViz("
digraph {

  graph [layout = dot, rankdir = TB]

  node [shape = rectangle, style = rounded]

  A [label='Start']
  B [label='Load BBL JSON Data']
  C [label='Data Cleaning & Standardisation']
  D [label='Ball-by-Ball Dataset']
  E [label='13-Over Snapshot Features\\n(Runs, Wickets, RRR, etc.)']

  F1 [label='Part A: Baseline Model\\n(No additional features)']
  F2 [label='Part B: Feature Engineering']

  G [label='Player Statistics']
  H [label='Role Classification']
  I [label='Weight Calculation\\n(W_batter, W_bowler)']
  J [label='RBD & RSO']

  K [label='Final Dataset']

  L1 [label='Part A Models\\n(Logistic, Tree)']
  L2 [label='Part B Models\\n(Logistic, Tree)']

  M [label='Evaluation\\n(Accuracy, ROC, AUC)']
  N [label='End']

  A -> B -> C -> D -> E

  E -> F1
  E -> F2

  F1 -> L1

  F2 -> G -> H -> I -> J -> K -> L2

  L1 -> M
  L2 -> M

  M -> N
}
")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# --- Table 1: Summary Statistics of RBD and RSO ---
snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  summarise(
    mean_RBD = mean(RBD, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_RBD   = sd(RBD,   na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_RSO = mean(RSO, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_RSO   = sd(RSO,   na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    digits  = 3,
    caption = "Summary Statistics of RBD and RSO",
    col.names = c("Mean RBD", "SD RBD", "Mean RSO", "SD RSO"),
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align    = c("r", "r", "r", "r")
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_classic(full_width = FALSE, html_font = "serif")
```



```{r}


# --- Table 2: Feature Comparison by Match Outcome ---
snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  mutate(outcome = ifelse(match_winner == 1, "Won", "Lost")) %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_RBD = mean(RBD,               na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_RSO = mean(RSO,               na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_RRR = mean(required_run_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    digits  = 3,
    caption = "Feature Comparison by Match Outcome",
    col.names = c("Outcome", "Avg RBD", "Avg RSO", "Avg RRR"),
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align    = c("l", "r", "r", "r")
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_classic(full_width = FALSE, html_font = "serif")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# --- Table 1: Summary Statistics of RBD and RSO (with Pearson correlations) ---
snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  summarise(
    mean_RBD  = mean(RBD, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_RBD    = sd(RBD,   na.rm = TRUE),
    cor_RBD   = cor(RBD, match_winner, use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson"),
    mean_RSO  = mean(RSO, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_RSO    = sd(RSO,   na.rm = TRUE),
    cor_RSO   = cor(RSO, match_winner, use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson")
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    digits    = 3,
    caption   = "Summary Statistics and Pearson Correlation with Match Outcome",
    col.names = c("Mean RBD", "SD RBD", "r (RBD)", "Mean RSO", "SD RSO", "r (RSO)"),
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    align     = c("r", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r")
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_classic(full_width = FALSE, html_font = "serif") %>%
  add_header_above(c("Remaining Batting Depth" = 3, "Remaining Specialist Overs" = 3))


# --- Table 2: Feature Comparison by Match Outcome (with Pearson correlations) ---

# Pre-compute correlations (single values, not group-level)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# --- Compute Pearson correlations LIVE from data ---
cor_RBD <- cor(snapshot_df_enhanced$RBD, 
               snapshot_df_enhanced$match_winner,
               use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson")

cor_RSO <- cor(snapshot_df_enhanced$RSO, 
               snapshot_df_enhanced$match_winner,
               use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson")

cor_RRR <- cor(snapshot_df_enhanced$required_run_rate, 
               snapshot_df_enhanced$match_winner,
               use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson")

# --- Build group summary (filter NA match_winner FIRST) ---
outcome_table <- snapshot_df_enhanced %>%
  filter(!is.na(match_winner)) %>%          # <-- THIS removes the NA row
  mutate(outcome = ifelse(match_winner == 1, "Won", "Lost")) %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_RBD = median(RBD,               na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_RSO = median(RSO,               na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_RRR = median(required_run_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
)%>%
  mutate(across(c(avg_RBD, avg_RSO, avg_RRR), ~ round(., 3)),
         across(everything(), as.character))

# --- Append Pearson r row ---
cor_row <- tibble(
  outcome = "Pearson r",
  avg_RBD = as.character(round(cor_RBD, 3)),
  avg_RSO = as.character(round(cor_RSO, 3)),
  avg_RRR = as.character(round(cor_RRR, 3))
)

outcome_table %>%
  bind_rows(cor_row) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    caption   = "Feature Comparison by Match Outcome with Pearson Correlation",
    col.names = c("Outcome", "Median_RBD", "Median_RSO", "Median_Req. Run Rate"),
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    align     = c("l", "r", "r", "r")
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_classic(full_width = FALSE, html_font = "serif") %>%
  row_spec(3, italic = TRUE, color = "gray40")

```

  


```{r data_leakage_demonstration}
# ============================================================
# DATA LEAKAGE DEMONSTRATION
# ============================================================
# The original W_batter and W_bowler are computed from ALL matches
# a player has ever appeared in, including the match being predicted.
# This means that when we evaluate a match, the player weights
# already "know" what happened in that match — a form of data leakage.
#
# To demonstrate this, we:
#   1. Randomly sample 5 matches
#   2. For each sampled match, get the list of players in that match
#   3. Recompute W_batter and W_bowler *excluding* that match's stats
#   4. Compare the leave-one-out weights to the original (leaked) weights
# ============================================================

set.seed(99)

# ---- Step 1: Sample 5 match IDs ----
sample_match_ids <- sample(unique(balls$match_id), 5)
cat("Sampled match IDs:\n"); print(sample_match_ids)

# ---- Helper: compute leave-one-out W_batter and W_bowler ----
compute_loo_weights <- function(focal_match) {

  # Batting summary excluding focal match
  bat_loo <- balls %>%
    filter(match_id != focal_match) %>%
    group_by(match_id, batter) %>%
    summarise(
      balls_faced = n(),
      runs        = sum(runs_batter, na.rm = TRUE),
      fours       = sum(runs_batter == 4, na.rm = TRUE),
      sixes       = sum(runs_batter == 6, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    group_by(player = batter) %>%
    summarise(
      balls_faced = sum(balls_faced),
      runs        = sum(runs),
      fours       = sum(fours),
      sixes       = sum(sixes),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Bowling summary excluding focal match
  bowl_loo <- balls %>%
    filter(match_id != focal_match) %>%
    group_by(match_id, bowler) %>%
    summarise(
      balls_bowled  = n(),
      wickets       = sum(wicket & !is.na(wicket_player_out), na.rm = TRUE),
      runs_conceded = sum(runs_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    group_by(player = bowler) %>%
    summarise(
      balls_bowled  = sum(balls_bowled),
      wickets       = sum(wickets),
      runs_conceded = sum(runs_conceded),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Batting position excluding focal match
  bat_pos_loo <- balls %>%
    filter(match_id != focal_match, innings %in% c(1, 2)) %>%
    group_by(match_id, innings, batter) %>%
    summarise(first_ball = min(delivery), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(match_id, innings) %>%
    mutate(batting_position = rank(first_ball, ties.method = "first")) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    group_by(player = batter) %>%
    summarise(
      avg_batting_position = round(mean(batting_position, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      position_weight = pmax(pmin((11 - avg_batting_position) / 10, 1), 0)
    )

  # Combine and compute LOO weights
  ps_loo <- full_join(bat_loo, bowl_loo, by = "player") %>%
    replace_na(list(
      balls_faced = 0, runs = 0, fours = 0, sixes = 0,
      balls_bowled = 0, wickets = 0, runs_conceded = 0
    )) %>%
    left_join(bat_pos_loo %>% select(player, avg_batting_position, position_weight),
              by = "player") %>%
    mutate(
      position_weight = replace_na(position_weight, 0),
      strike_rate     = ifelse(balls_faced > 0, (runs / balls_faced) * 100, NA_real_),
      sr_position_raw = case_when(
        balls_faced > 0 ~ strike_rate * position_weight,
        TRUE            ~ 0
      ),
      W_batter_loo = case_when(
        balls_faced > 0 ~
          (sr_position_raw - min(sr_position_raw[balls_faced > 0], na.rm = TRUE)) /
          (max(sr_position_raw[balls_faced > 0], na.rm = TRUE) -
           min(sr_position_raw[balls_faced > 0], na.rm = TRUE)),
        balls_faced == 0 ~ 0
      ),
      economy_rate     = ifelse(balls_bowled > 0, (runs_conceded / balls_bowled) * 6, NA_real_),
      bowling_sr       = ifelse(wickets > 0, balls_bowled / wickets, NA_real_),
      economy_score    = ifelse(balls_bowled > 0, max(economy_rate, na.rm = TRUE) - economy_rate, 0),
      bowling_sr_score = ifelse(!is.na(bowling_sr), max(bowling_sr, na.rm = TRUE) - bowling_sr, 0),
      econ_norm_loo = ifelse(
        balls_bowled > 0,
        (economy_score - min(economy_score[balls_bowled > 0], na.rm = TRUE)) /
        (max(economy_score[balls_bowled > 0], na.rm = TRUE) -
         min(economy_score[balls_bowled > 0], na.rm = TRUE)),
        0
      ),
      sr_norm_loo = ifelse(
        !is.na(bowling_sr),
        (bowling_sr_score - min(bowling_sr_score[!is.na(bowling_sr)], na.rm = TRUE)) /
        (max(bowling_sr_score[!is.na(bowling_sr)], na.rm = TRUE) -
         min(bowling_sr_score[!is.na(bowling_sr)], na.rm = TRUE)),
        0
      ),
      W_bowler_loo = 0.5 * econ_norm_loo + 0.5 * sr_norm_loo
    ) %>%
    select(player, W_batter_loo, W_bowler_loo)

  # Get players in the focal match and join original weights
  focal_players <- playing_xi %>%
    filter(match_id == focal_match) %>%
    pull(player)

  ps_loo %>%
    filter(player %in% focal_players) %>%
    left_join(player_stats %>% select(player, W_batter, W_bowler), by = "player") %>%
    mutate(
      match_id       = focal_match,
      delta_W_batter = W_batter_loo - W_batter,
      delta_W_bowler = W_bowler_loo - W_bowler
    ) %>%
    select(match_id, player,
           W_batter, W_batter_loo, delta_W_batter,
           W_bowler, W_bowler_loo, delta_W_bowler)
}

# ---- Step 2: Run for all 5 sampled matches ----
leakage_comparison <- map_dfr(sample_match_ids, compute_loo_weights)

# ---- Step 3: Per-match summary table ----
leakage_comparison %>%
  group_by(match_id) %>%
  summarise(
    n_players            = n(),
    mean_delta_W_batter  = round(mean(delta_W_batter,        na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    sd_delta_W_batter    = round(sd(delta_W_batter,          na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    max_abs_delta_batter = round(max(abs(delta_W_batter),    na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    mean_delta_W_bowler  = round(mean(delta_W_bowler,        na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    sd_delta_W_bowler    = round(sd(delta_W_bowler,          na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    max_abs_delta_bowler = round(max(abs(delta_W_bowler),    na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    caption   = "Per-match average weight shift: LOO vs Original (non-zero = data leakage confirmed)",
    col.names = c("Match ID", "Players",
                  "Mean ΔW_bat", "SD ΔW_bat", "Max |ΔW_bat|",
                  "Mean ΔW_bowl", "SD ΔW_bowl", "Max |ΔW_bowl|"),
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    align     = c("l", rep("c", 7))
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_classic(full_width = FALSE, html_font = "serif")

# ---- Step 4: Full player-level table ----
leakage_comparison %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 4))) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    caption   = "Player-level W_batter and W_bowler: Original (with leakage) vs Leave-One-Out",
    col.names = c("Match ID", "Player",
                  "W_bat (orig)", "W_bat (LOO)", "ΔW_bat",
                  "W_bowl (orig)", "W_bowl (LOO)", "ΔW_bowl"),
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    align     = c("l", "l", rep("c", 6))
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_classic(full_width = FALSE, html_font = "serif") %>%
  kableExtra::collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")

# ---- Step 5: Histogram of delta distributions ----
leakage_comparison %>%
  select(match_id, player, delta_W_batter, delta_W_bowler) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(delta_W_batter, delta_W_bowler),
    names_to  = "metric",
    values_to = "delta"
  ) %>%
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
                         delta_W_batter = "ΔW_batter",
                         delta_W_bowler = "ΔW_bowler")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = delta, fill = metric)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("ΔW_batter" = "#1D9E75", "ΔW_bowler" = "#378ADD")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Data Leakage: Weight shift when focal match is excluded (LOO vs Original)",
    subtitle = "Non-zero deltas confirm that including the focal match inflates/deflates player weights",
    x        = "Δ Weight (LOO − Original)",
    y        = "Number of players"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

# ---- Step 6: Scatter — Original vs LOO ----
p_batter <- ggplot(leakage_comparison, aes(x = W_batter, y = W_batter_loo, colour = match_id)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  labs(
    title    = "W_batter: Original vs Leave-One-Out",
    subtitle = "Deviation from diagonal = leakage-induced bias",
    x        = "W_batter (original — includes focal match)",
    y        = "W_batter (LOO — focal match excluded)",
    colour   = "Match ID"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))

p_bowler <- ggplot(leakage_comparison, aes(x = W_bowler, y = W_bowler_loo, colour = match_id)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  labs(
    title    = "Bowler Weightage: Original vs Leave-One-Out",
    subtitle = "Deviation from diagonal = leakage-induced bias",
    x        = "Bowler Weightage (original — includes focal match)",
    y        = "Bowler Weightage (LOO — focal match excluded)",
    colour   = "Match ID"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10))

print(p_batter)
print(p_bowler)

# ---- Step 7: Interpretation ----
cat(
  "\n=== INTERPRETATION ===\n",
  "Non-zero ΔW values confirm data leakage: a player's weight was influenced\n",
  "by the very match being predicted. The effect is largest for players with\n",
  "fewer career appearances, where one match is a large share of career data.\n",
  "A proper pipeline should recompute W_batter and W_bowler using only\n",
  "matches PRIOR to each focal match.\n"
)
```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 7: W_batter Distribution by Role (normalised y) ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(batter_weights %>% filter(role != "Other"), aes(x = W_batter, y = after_stat(density), fill = role)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~role, ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Batter" = "#1D9E75", "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27", "Bowler" = "#378ADD")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Batter weightage distribution (W_batter) by role",
    subtitle = "Batters/Allrounders normalised by SR | Bowlers: 0 if never batted",
    x        = "Batter weight (0 = no batting contribution, 1 = highest SR)",
    y        = "Density (proportion within role)",
    fill     = "Role"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# ============================================================
# ---- FIGURE 8: W_bowler Distribution by Role (normalised y) ----
# ============================================================
ggplot(player_stats %>% filter(role != "Other"), aes(x = W_bowler, y = after_stat(density), fill = role)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~role, ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Bowler" = "#378ADD", "Allrounder" = "#EF9F27", "Batter" = "#1D9E75")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Bowler weightage distribution (W_bowler) by role",
    subtitle = "Economy (50%) + bowling SR (50%) | 0 = never bowled, 1 = strongest bowler",
    x        = "Bowler weight (0 = no bowling contribution, 1 = strongest)",
    y        = "Density (proportion within role)",
    fill     = "Role"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 10),
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```
